public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as tax witness.
manner: Thinks in testimony, signatures, and remembered sequence, even when everyone else wants to move faster. In The Union, that reads as someone deal-aware, balanced, and procedurally diplomatic, with an eye on leverage and precedent, and the habits of a tax witness still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of tax witness in and around Union Successor Heartland, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Union Successor Heartland workable through the obligations of tax witness while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in The Union.
hidden_pressure: Must preserve sequence and custody even when powerful parties would prefer ambiguity. Around Union Successor Heartland, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of tax witness visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Taren March can trade precise, actionable information once the party secures or clarifies the record they protect.
Taren March needs help resolving a missing ledger, inconsistent testimony, or tampered record tied to Union Successor Heartland.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Seals, ledgers, or carefully bundled papers make record authority the first thing Taren March communicates in public.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Union Successor Heartland: offices, toll points, ledgers, archives, or testimony desks
who_avoids_them: those hoping wording can stay vague
